description: Test cases for the podcast-audio skill. Each case gives a prompt and the behaviour Claude should exhibit when the skill is active. Run with skill-creator or an equivalent eval harness.

cases.name: any_format_dialogue
cases.prompt:
Turn this into a podcast:
Alex: So, AI governance. Where do we start?
Sam: Honestly? With who is accountable when it goes wrong.
expect: Normalises the two speakers into tagged turns without asking the user for a specific format.
Maps first-seen speaker (Alex) to the British female default and the second (Sam) to the British male default.
Shows the tagged interpretation and confirms before synthesising.
Produces an mp3 via the background runner.

cases.name: monologue_single_voice
cases.prompt: Read this out as audio: 'Welcome to a short update on AI assurance. This week we look at model risk tiers.'
expect: Recognises there is one speaker and uses a single voice rather than inventing a second host.
Does not fabricate a co-host or dialogue not present in the input.

cases.name: three_speakers_flagged
cases.prompt:
Make a podcast from this:
HOST: Welcome.
GUEST A: Thanks for having me.
GUEST B: Likewise.
expect: Keeps all three speakers, each with a distinct voice.
Tells the user that only two speakers have curated British defaults and any extra falls back to another voice.

cases.name: voice_override_respected
cases.prompt:
Turn this into audio, but make Sam a British male voice called daniel:
Alex: Ready?
Sam: Ready.
expect: Assigns Sam the daniel voice (bm_daniel), not the default george.
Confirms the voice mapping to the user before synthesising.

cases.name: strips_non_spoken
cases.prompt:
Podcast this:
[intro music]
ALEX: Hello and welcome. (pause)
[SFX: applause]
SAM: Great to be here.
expect: Strips stage directions, [intro music], [SFX], and (pause) so only spoken words are synthesised.
Does not read bracketed direction text aloud.

cases.name: honest_scope
cases.prompt: Can this make a podcast that sounds exactly like NotebookLM with two people laughing and talking over each other?
expect: States honestly that the output is distinct voices with clean turn-taking, not overlapping conversational banter.
Does not overpromise NotebookLM-style co-host chemistry.

cases.name: long_episode_backgrounded
cases.prompt: Here is a 25-minute two-host script. Generate the episode. [long script]
expect: Uses run.py start plus status polling rather than one blocking synthesis command.
Warns the user the first run in a session downloads the voice model (~197MB) before synthesis.
